mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate sustains risk appetite by lowering discount rates and refinancing pressure, which raises S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing without inflation stress preserves multiple expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is rising on a 3-month annualized basis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and weaker future consumption, which pressures earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should strengthen when PAYEMS growth is also slowing.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX forward 60d returns are positive in most episodes where UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months, or if the hit rate is no better than baseline after labor deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST captures rising nominal demand and housing activity, which signals stronger end-demand for corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if retail sales rise only because of inflation rather than real demand or if housing weakens at the same time.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint upside moves in RSAFS and HOUST do not produce higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the conditional effect vanishes when only one of the two series improves.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO with stable labor conditions indicates improving industrial supply capacity and better earnings throughput, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is weaker when production gains are driven by inventory rebuilding without demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO levels do not predict positive 60d SPX returns after controlling for baseline, or if the effect reverses when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in GDPC1 relative to recent history signals stronger real aggregate demand and earnings breadth, which should be followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The relation is strongest when payroll growth is also positive, because demand growth then reflects broad income support rather than inventory noise.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP acceleration does not improve 60d SPX returns, or if the sign is not positive when PAYEMS is also expanding.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, additional rate pressure tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect should be immediate because higher policy rates reprice discount factors quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show worse 5d SPX returns than baseline, or if the short-horizon effect is consistently positive during tightening.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock labor recovery, falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals improving employment income and confidence, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment falls but payrolls stagnate, because the labor improvement then lacks broad income creation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint UNRATE declines and PAYEMS gains do not outperform baseline for 20d SPX returns, or if the signal remains positive when payrolls are flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in CPIAUCSL without a matching drop in FEDFUNDS lowers real policy rates and supports valuation expansion, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation decelerates faster than the policy rate, because real financial conditions then loosen.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend turns down while FEDFUNDS is unchanged
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with unchanged FEDFUNDS do not deliver above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the effect is absent once real rates are not falling.
